evidence_quotes.quote: tactics like FAFO (Fear of Missing out), microtransaction
Video games studios like Nintendo turn off games on purpose so that you ARE FORCED to buy their next game and you cannot play OR BUY their "old" games.

paragraphs.text: Hi, i would kindly, personally as a consumer and as resident of Europe and member of European Union to have a protection from publishers to turn off or remove access or ability to play games that i already purchase with my money. It is really anti-consumer behavior when a game publisher turn off game and take my money. I have some personal experience with games like Mario Cart 8 and most of Nintendo games that are impossible to officially and legally play. Same goes with Electronic Arts (EA) games like FIFA games, Need For Speed games, NBA games and so on. Video games studios like Nintendo turn off games on purpose so that you ARE FORCED to buy their next game and you cannot play OR BUY their "old" games, they are always removed for capitalistic reasons. Obviously, they are not the only one, Electronic Arts are doing the same with FIFA games. I personally don't want to play their new multiplayer games because they are famous for using tactics like FAFO (Fear of Missing out), microtransaction ,let be honest, not they are "MAXItransaction" for how much money they want for digital "goods". Personally, mostly i am invested in game World of Tanks. They do not have any End of Life plan but in their case, they are holding on for really long time. There are many games that are already destroyed or at risk being destroyed. There is a long list of games that i added in attached file. Some games are preserved by fans of the game but god now for how long. There are at least 358 games dead or unsupported, 389 at risk to be dead soon in the future and 209 are fan preserved. But those are only multiplayer games. There are many more games (probably in thousands) for single player games that disappeared and are lost to time and space. Please, protect my consumer rights for OWNING and USING or playing games that i PURCHASED. I want to own things, my own tools, my own devices, MY OWN GAMES. I will NOT GET COMFORTABLE not owning my games like Ubisoft Executive Philippe Tremblay threaten that i will not own their games. Ubisoft is acting very anti-consumer. Overall i don't want a government to have a complete over-reach of controlling games but there is a need of some reach because game publishers are getting too comfortable. Beside that, i don't want payment processor to dictate what i can or cannot buy, they are not the law. Also i don't want to use my ID on the internet to access ANY KIND of websites.
